AccountingModelCard.carddescription:
Paperclip now separates request-level inference usage from account-level finance events.
          That keeps provider reporting honest when the biller is OpenRouter, Cloudflare, Bedrock, or another intermediary.
AccountingModelCard.description: Request-scoped usage and billed runs from cost_events.
AccountingModelCard.description_1: Account-level charges that are not one prompt-response pair.
AccountingModelCard.description_2: Provider or biller windows that can stop traffic in real time.
AccountingModelCard.title: Inference ledger

deployment.localTrusted: Local trusted mode is optimized for a local operator. Browser requests run as local board context and no sign-in is required.
deployment.private: Authenticated private mode requires sign-in and is intended for LAN, VPN, or other private-network deployments.
deployment.public: Authenticated public mode requires sign-in for board access and is intended for public URLs.

general.aiFeedbackSharingDescription: Control whether thumbs up and thumbs down votes can send the voted AI output to Paperclip Labs. Votes are always saved locally.
general.backupRetentionDescription: Configure how long automatic database backups are retained. Backups run roughly every hour and are compressed with gzip. Within the daily window all backups are kept; beyond that, one backup per week and one per month are preserved.
general.censorUsernameDescription: Hide the username segment in home-directory paths and similar operator-visible log output. Standalone username mentions outside of paths are not yet masked in the live transcript view. This is off by default.
general.description: Configure instance-wide preferences including log display, keyboard shortcuts, backup retention, and data sharing.
general.dontAllowDescription: Keep voted AI outputs local only.
general.keyboardShortcutsDescription: Enable app keyboard shortcuts, including inbox navigation and global shortcuts like creating issues or toggling panels. This is off by default.
general.noDefaultSaved: No default is saved yet. The next thumbs up or thumbs down choice will ask once and then save the answer here.
general.retestHint: To retest the first-use prompt in local dev, remove the {{key}} key from the {{table}} JSON row for this instance, or set it back to {{value}}. Unset and {{value}} both mean no default has been chosen yet.
general.signOutDescription: Sign out of this Paperclip instance. You will be redirected to the login page.
general.title: General

connectivityBlock.noCandidates:
No candidate URLs are available. Ask your user to configure a reachable hostname in Paperclip, then retry.
Suggested steps:
- choose a hostname that resolves to the Paperclip host from your runtime
- run: pnpm paperclipai allowed-hostname <host>
- restart Paperclip
- verify with: curl -fsS http://<host>:3100/api/health
- regenerate this invite snippet
connectivityBlock.someCandidates:
If none are reachable, ask your user to add a reachable hostname in Paperclip, restart, and retry.
Suggested command:
- pnpm paperclipai allowed-hostname <host>
Then verify with: curl -fsS <base-url>/api/health

InstanceExperimentalSettings.div:
No recovery tasks would be created right now. Auto-recovery can still run for future liveness incidents in
              this window.
InstanceExperimentalSettings.label: Instance Settings
InstanceExperimentalSettings.p: Opt into features that are still being evaluated before they become default behavior.
InstanceExperimentalSettings.p_1:
Show environment management in company settings and allow project and agent environment assignment
              controls.
InstanceExperimentalSettings.p_2:
Show execution workspace controls in project configuration and allow isolated workspace behavior for new
              and existing issue runs.
InstanceExperimentalSettings.p_3:
In `pnpm dev:once`, wait for all queued and running local agent runs to finish, then restart the server
              automatically when backend changes or migrations make the current boot stale.
InstanceExperimentalSettings.p_4:
Let the heartbeat scheduler create recovery issues for issue dependency chains found inside the
                configured lookback window.

InstanceSettings.label: Instance Settings
InstanceSettings.p: Agents with a timer heartbeat enabled across all of your companies.

IssueChatLongThreadPerf.label: Fixture rows
IssueChatLongThreadPerf.p:
Deterministic local fixture for measuring the current direct-render issue chat path with
            hundreds of merged thread rows, markdown-heavy assistant bodies, linked runs, documents,
            sub-issues, and sidebar context.

IssueChatUxLab.carddescription: This route should be the fastest way to inspect the chat system before or after tweaks.
IssueChatUxLab.cardtitle: What to evaluate on this page
IssueChatUxLab.check_that_user_assistant_and_system_row: Check that user, assistant, and system rows scan differently without feeling like separate products.
IssueChatUxLab.description: Isolated ticker that cycles sample reasoning lines on a timer. The outgoing line slides up and fades out while the incoming line slides up from below. Runs in a loop so you can tune timing and easing without needing a live stream.
IssueChatUxLab.description_1: The "Working" token uses the shimmer-text gradient sweep to signal an active run. Once the run completes it becomes the static "Worked" token.
IssueChatUxLab.description_2: Shows the fully active state: timeline events, historical run marker, a running assistant reply with reasoning and tools, and a queued follow-up from the user.
IssueChatUxLab.description_3: When a user sends a message, the bubble briefly shows a "Sending..." label at reduced opacity until the server confirms receipt. This preview renders that transient state.
IssueChatUxLab.description_4: Shows the post-run state: assistant comment feedback controls, historical run context, and timeline reassignment without any active stream.
IssueChatUxLab.description_5: Keeps the message area visible even when there is no thread yet, and replaces the composer with an explicit warning when replies are blocked.
IssueChatUxLab.p:
This page exercises the real assistant-ui issue chat with fixture-backed messages. Use it to review
              spacing, chronology, running states, tool rendering, activity rows, queueing, and composer behavior
              without needing a live issue in progress.
IssueChatUxLab.title: Rotating reasoning text
IssueChatUxLab.watch_the_live_preview_for_reasoning_den: Watch the live preview for reasoning density, tool expansion behavior, and queued follow-up readability.

ProjectWorkspaceDetail.description: A local path Paperclip can use directly.
ProjectWorkspaceDetail.description_1: A local folder without git semantics.
ProjectWorkspaceDetail.description_2: A repo URL with optional refs and local checkout.
ProjectWorkspaceDetail.description_3: A hosted workspace tracked by external reference.
ProjectWorkspaceDetail.label: Local git checkout
ProjectWorkspaceDetail.p_1: Workspace not found for this project.
ProjectWorkspaceDetail.p_2:
Configure the concrete workspace Paperclip attaches to this project. These values drive per-workspace
                  checkout behavior, default runtime services for child execution workspaces, and let you override setup
                  or cleanup commands when one workspace needs special handling.
ProjectWorkspaceDetail.p_3: Paperclip derives Services and Jobs from this JSON. Prefer editing named commands first; use raw JSON for advanced lifecycle, port, readiness, or environment settings.
ProjectWorkspaceDetail.p_5: Long-running services stay supervised here, while one-shot jobs run on demand against this workspace. Execution workspaces inherit this config unless they override it.
ProjectWorkspaceDetail.this_is_the_project_s_primary_codebase_w: This is the project’s primary codebase workspace.
